diff:
diff --git a/.github/labeler.yml b/.github/labeler.yml
index 0db3be2..b3cfb51 100644
--- a/.github/labeler.yml
+++ b/.github/labeler.yml
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 # changes to documentation generation
 "area/docs-generation": 
 - changed-files:
-  - any-glob-to-any-file: 'doc/**'
+  - any-glob-to-any-file: ['doc/**', 'site/**']
 
 # changes to the core cobra command
 "area/cobra-command":
@@ -22,3 +22,8 @@
 "area/shell-completion":
 - changed-files:
   - any-glob-to-any-file: './*completions*'
+
+# changes to flag groups and active help
+"area/flag-groups":
+- changed-files:
+  - any-glob-to-any-file: ['./flag_groups.go', './active_help.go']
diff --git a/MAINTAINERS b/MAINTAINERS
index 4c5ac3d..118649b 100644
--- a/MAINTAINERS
+++ b/MAINTAINERS
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ maintainers:
 - johnSchnake
 - jpmcb
 - marckhouzam
-inactive:
+emeritus:
 - anthonyfok
 - bep
 - bogem
 - broady
 - eparis
 - jharshman
 - wfernandes

solve_notes: Extended the `area/docs-generation` glob list in `.github/labeler.yml` to include `site/**` in addition to `doc/**`, added a new `area/flag-groups` label rule matching `flag_groups.go` and `active_help.go`, and renamed the `inactive:` key to `emeritus:` in `MAINTAINERS`.
task_text:
Fix .github/labeler.yml not labeling PRs that modify the site/ content

The `.github/labeler.yml` defines label rules for file path patterns:
`area/docs-generation` matches `doc/**`, `area/shell-completion` matches
`./*completions*`, `area/cobra-command` matches command files, and
`area/flags` matches `args*.go`. However, there is no label covering
`site/**` (the Hugo-based documentation site content under
`site/content/`), so PRs that add or modify user-guide pages or
example content receive no label. Update `.github/labeler.yml` to
extend the `area/docs-generation` rule to also match `site/**`, and
add a new `area/flag-groups` label rule matching `flag_groups.go` and
`active_help.go` (currently unlabeled). Update the `MAINTAINERS` file
to add an `emeritus` section (rename the existing `inactive:` key to
`emeritus:`) for consistency with common open-source maintainer file
conventions.
tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum_sufficient set contains all three changed config entries. thrash_preventing is empty because these are pure config/text edits — no code logic, no callers, no type signatures that an agent needs to check.
